trm:term.trm:name: Python Binding
trm:term.trm:definition: A PyO3-based native extension that wraps the Rust clayers crates as a Python package. The binding translates Rust types into Python classes, maps Rust errors to a typed exception hierarchy, and bridges the async/sync divide between the tokio-based clayers-repo and Python's asyncio. Published as the "clayers" package on PyPI.

trm:term.trm:name: Knowledge Model
trm:term.trm:definition: The Python class that represents a loaded clayers specification. Wraps spec discovery, validation, drift detection, coverage analysis, connectivity analysis, hash fixing, and XPath querying behind a single stateful object. Constructed from a filesystem path pointing to a spec directory.

trm:term.trm:name: Queryable Protocol
trm:term.trm:definition: A Python typing.Protocol that both KnowledgeModel and Repo implement, defining a common query(xpath, mode) interface. Enables generic Python functions to accept either a spec or a repository as a query target, unifying the two query surfaces.

trm:term.trm:name: RepoInner
trm:term.trm:definition: An enum-dispatch wrapper around the generic Repo<S> type, since PyO3 does not support generic pyclasses. RepoInner holds either Repo<MemoryStore> or Repo<SqliteStore>, wrapped in Arc for sharing between sync and async Python classes. A dispatch macro routes method calls to the correct variant.

trm:term.trm:name: Global Tokio Runtime
trm:term.trm:definition: A lazily-initialized, shared tokio multi-thread runtime used by the sync Repo class to bridge from synchronous Python calls to the async clayers-repo methods. Created once via OnceLock and reused across all sync Repo instances. The sync class releases the Python GIL via Python.detach() before calling block_on(), preventing deadlocks between the GIL and the tokio runtime.

pr:section.pr:title: Python Integration
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: PyO3 bindings exposing the clayers crates as a native Python package with both sync and async APIs.
pr:section.pr:p: The clayers-py crate provides Python bindings to all four Rust crates: clayers-xml (content hashing, canonicalization), clayers-spec (validation, drift, coverage, connectivity, querying), clayers-repo (version-controlled XML repositories), and the CLI-level assembly logic. The result is a single Python package, import clayers, that enables Python tooling to work with specs and repositories natively without shelling out to the CLI.
The binding is built with PyO3 and packaged via maturin. The native extension module is clayers._clayers, and pure Python wrapper modules provide re-exports, protocols, and submodule structure.

Typical use cases include: CI pipelines that validate specs and fail on drift, pytest suites that assert coverage thresholds, Jupyter notebooks that explore spec connectivity, web dashboards that render spec health metrics, and agent frameworks that query specs programmatically instead of parsing CLI output.

pr:section.pr:title: Module Structure
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: How the Python package is organized into submodules and re-exports.
pr:section.pr:p: The package follows a layered structure mirroring the Rust crates:

Users switch between sync and async by changing the import path. The sync from clayers.repo import Repo and async from clayers.repo.aio import Repo share the same class name and method signatures, making them a drop-in swap.

pr:section.pr:title: KnowledgeModel API
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: The central class for working with clayers specs from Python.
pr:section.pr:p: The KnowledgeModel class is constructed from a filesystem path and eagerly discovers index files and spec files. Each method returns a typed result object that mirrors the corresponding Rust struct with read-only Python attributes. Errors are raised as SpecError exceptions.

py:doc.pr:p: Compare stored hashes against current content.
Graph metrics: components, hubs, bridges, cycles.
Recompute spec-side hashes in artifact mappings.
Recompute code-side hashes in artifact mappings.

pr:section.pr:p: A CI gate that validates a spec and checks drift on every commit:
pr:section.pr:codeblock:
from clayers import KnowledgeModel
km = KnowledgeModel("clayers/my-project") result = km.validate() assert result.is_valid, [e.message for e in result.errors]
drift = km.check_drift() assert drift.drifted_count == 0, ( f"{drift.drifted_count} mappings drifted" )
pr:section.pr:p: Querying the spec as a knowledge base to find which code implements a concept:
pr:section.pr:codeblock:
km = KnowledgeModel("clayers/clayers")
# Find all terms terms = km.query("//trm:term/trm:name", mode="text") print(terms.values) # ['Layer', 'Namespace', 'Node', ...]
# Find artifact mappings for a concept paths = km.query( '//art:mapping[art:spec-ref/@node="term-drift"]' '/art:artifact/@path', mode="text", ) print(paths.values) # ['crates/clayers-spec/src/drift.rs']
pr:section.pr:p: A coverage threshold test in pytest:
pr:section.pr:codeblock: def test_spec_coverage_above_threshold(): km = KnowledgeModel("clayers/my-project") cov = km.coverage() mapped_pct = cov.mapped_nodes / cov.total_nodes * 100 assert mapped_pct >= 80, ( f"Only {mapped_pct:.0f}% of spec nodes are mapped. " f"Unmapped: {cov.unmapped_nodes}" )
pr:section.pr:p: Exploring spec connectivity in a notebook:
pr:section.pr:codeblock: km = KnowledgeModel("clayers/clayers") conn = km.connectivity() print(f"Nodes: {conn.node_count}, Edges: {conn.edge_count}") print(f"Density: {conn.density:.4f}") for hub in conn.hub_nodes[:3]: print(f" Hub: {hub.id} (degree {hub.total_degree})") for node_id in conn.isolated_nodes[:5]: print(f" Isolated: {node_id}")

pr:section.pr:title: Repository API
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Sync and async Python wrappers for the content-addressed XML repository.
pr:section.pr:p: The repository API wraps clayers-repo's Repo<S> behind RepoInner enum dispatch. Both the sync and async Repo classes hold an Arc<RepoInner> and expose identical method signatures. The sync class uses a shared tokio runtime with block_on() after releasing the GIL. The async class returns Python awaitables via future_into_py().

py:doc.pr:p: Decompose XML into the Merkle DAG, return document hash.
Reconstruct XML from its content hash.
Create commit on branch pointing to tree.

pr:section.pr:p: A version-controlled XML editing workflow using the sync API:
pr:section.pr:codeblock:
from clayers.repo import Repo, MemoryStore, Author
repo = Repo(MemoryStore()) author = Author("Alice", "alice@example.com")
# Import documents and commit spec = repo.import_xml("<spec><section id='intro'/></spec>") index = repo.import_xml("<index><file href='spec.xml'/></index>") tree = repo.build_tree([ ("spec.xml", spec), ("index.xml", index), ]) commit = repo.commit("main", tree, author, "Initial spec")
# Later: modify, commit, and diff spec_v2 = repo.import_xml("<spec><section id='intro' status='draft'/></spec>") tree_v2 = repo.build_tree([("spec.xml", spec_v2), ("index.xml", index)]) commit_v2 = repo.commit("main", tree_v2, author, "Add draft status")
changes = repo.diff_trees(tree, tree_v2) for c in changes: print(f"{c.kind}: {c.path}")
pr:section.pr:p: The same workflow using the async API (only the import changes):
pr:section.pr:codeblock:
from clayers.repo.aio import Repo # only this line changes from clayers.repo import MemoryStore, Author
repo = Repo(MemoryStore()) author = Author("Alice", "alice@example.com")
spec = await repo.import_xml("<spec/>") tree = await repo.build_tree([("spec.xml", spec)]) commit = await repo.commit("main", tree, author, "Initial")
pr:section.pr:p: Persistent storage with SQLite survives process restarts:
pr:section.pr:codeblock:
from clayers.repo import Repo, Author from clayers import SqliteStore
repo = Repo(SqliteStore.open("my-project.db")) # All commits, branches, tags persist to disk

pr:section.pr:title: Error Hierarchy
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: How Rust errors map to Python exceptions.
pr:section.pr:p: A base ClayersError exception inherits from Python's Exception. Three subclasses map to the Rust crate error types:

py:doc.pr:p: Base exception for all clayers errors.
Raised by KnowledgeModel constructor and all methods.
Raised by Repo constructor and all methods.

pr:section.pr:p: Catching errors at different levels of specificity:

pr:section.pr:title: Shared Query Protocol
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: How KnowledgeModel and Repo share the Queryable interface.
pr:section.pr:p: Both KnowledgeModel and Repo implement the Queryable protocol, a runtime-checkable typing.Protocol. This enables generic Python code to accept either as a query target.
Writing a function that works with both filesystem specs and versioned repositories:
pr:section.pr:codeblock:
from clayers import Queryable
def count_terms(source: Queryable) -> int: result = source.query("//trm:term", mode="count") return result.count or 0
# Works with specs on disk from clayers import KnowledgeModel km = KnowledgeModel("clayers/my-project") print(count_terms(km)) # e.g. 42
# Works identically with a repository snapshot from clayers.repo import Repo, MemoryStore repo = Repo(MemoryStore()) # ... after importing and committing documents ... print(count_terms(repo))

org:concept.org:purpose: Understanding the purpose and scope of the Python bindings and how they relate to the four Rust crates.

org:reference.org:purpose: Lookup reference for which Python module wraps which Rust crate and what types are available.
Reference for the KnowledgeModel class methods and return types.
Reference for the sync/async Repo API and the inner dispatch design.

org:concept.org:purpose: Understanding how Rust errors propagate as typed Python exceptions.
Understanding the shared query protocol and QueryResult type.
Core vocabulary for the Python integration.
The central Python class wrapping spec operations.
The shared query interface between KnowledgeModel and Repo.
The enum dispatch pattern for wrapping generic Rust types in PyO3.
The shared tokio runtime bridging sync Python to async Rust.

rel:relation.rel:note: The Python bindings expose the layered architecture to Python tooling, wrapping all four Rust crates.
KnowledgeModel assembles the combined document to answer queries.
KnowledgeModel.fix_artifact_hashes() and fix_node_hashes() operate on artifact mappings.
Both sync and async Repo hold Arc<RepoInner>.
The sync Repo class uses the global runtime to bridge async-to-sync.
The error hierarchy is part of the binding layer.
KnowledgeModel is the primary class in the Python integration, wrapping all spec analysis operations.
The Repo API is the other main class in the Python integration, wrapping VCS operations.
Module structure defines how the Python package is organized into importable submodules.
The Queryable protocol unifies KnowledgeModel and Repo query surfaces within the integration.
The API section documents the KnowledgeModel class.
The shared query section documents the Queryable protocol.

art:mapping.art:note: The #[pymodule] root wires all submodules together: errors, query, spec types, KnowledgeModel, xml, repo.
RepoInner enum (Memory/Sqlite variants), dispatch! macro, global tokio runtime via OnceLock, and SharedRepo type alias.

spec:clayers.art:exempt: Documentation section containing py:exception elements. Individual exceptions are artifact-mapped.
Documentation section describing the Queryable protocol. The QueryResult py:class is artifact-mapped.
Vocabulary term defining the Queryable protocol concept. The implementing code is mapped via py:class and py:method nodes.
spec:clayers.llm:node: The Python integration section describes the clayers-py crate: a PyO3 native extension that wraps all four Rust crates (clayers-xml, clayers-spec, clayers-repo, and CLI-level logic) as the "clayers" Python package. The binding provides KnowledgeModel for spec analysis, sync/async Repo for VCS operations, ContentHash for hash manipulation, and a typed exception hierarchy. Key design: enum dispatch for generic Repo<S>, shared global tokio runtime for sync, Queryable protocol unifying KnowledgeModel and Repo.

Module layout: clayers (re-exports), clayers.xml (ContentHash), clayers.repo (sync Repo, MemoryStore, SqliteStore, Author), clayers.repo.aio (async Repo). Native extension is clayers._clayers. Users swap sync/async by changing the import path only.

Describes the KnowledgeModel class using structured py: elements. py:class with py:property (name, files, combined_xml, schema_dir) and py:method (validate, check_drift, coverage, connectivity, query, fix_node_hashes, fix_artifact_hashes). Each method has typed params and return types. Prose examples show CI gates, queries, coverage thresholds, and connectivity exploration.

Describes the Repo API using structured py: elements. py:class for Repo (12 methods: import/export, trees, commits, branches, tags, log, diff, query) plus supporting py:class elements for Author, CommitObject, FileChange, ContentHash. Sync and async share the same signatures; switching is import-path only.
Describes the exception hierarchy using py:exception elements. ClayersError (base, extends Exception) with XmlError, SpecError, RepoError subclasses. Each maps to a Rust crate error type.

Describes the Queryable protocol and QueryResult using py: elements. py:class QueryResult with properties kind, count, values. The Queryable typing.Protocol unifies KnowledgeModel and Repo query APIs.
The clayers-py crate: PyO3 + maturin native extension. Wraps all Rust crates as Python classes. Module name: clayers._clayers. Pure Python wrappers provide re-exports and protocols.

Python class for loaded specs. Constructor discovers index/spec files. Methods: validate, check_drift, coverage, connectivity, fix_node_hashes, fix_artifact_hashes, query. All sync (clayers-spec is sync Rust).
typing.Protocol unifying KnowledgeModel and Repo query APIs. Single method: query(xpath, *, mode="xml") -> QueryResult. Runtime-checkable via isinstance().
Enum dispatch for PyO3: RepoInner::Memory(Repo<MemoryStore>) or RepoInner::Sqlite(Repo<SqliteStore>). Wrapped in Arc for sharing between sync/async classes. dispatch! macro routes calls.
OnceLock<tokio::runtime::Runtime> shared across all sync Repo instances. Multi-thread runtime with enable_all(). Sync methods call py.detach() to release GIL, then block_on() on this runtime.
